field_description: Classifies whether an instrument is a multi-question survey or a single-question platform poll. Community Pulse is a presentation of eligible poll questions, not a separate response type.

field_option.option_description: An instrument containing one or more ordered questions and ordinarily presented as a dedicated form.
A single-question instrument that may be presented through Community Pulse or another approved platform placement.

field_description: Defines the answer control and stored answer shape for one immutable survey-revision question.

field_option.option_description: Respondent selects exactly one option from a controlled list.
Respondent selects a bounded number of options from a controlled list.
Respondent selects one integer on the question's defined scale, normally 1 through 10.
Respondent places controlled options into a preferred order.
Respondent selects one controlled option represented by an accessible image and text label.
Respondent selects Yes or No; use only when the question genuinely has two exhaustive answers.
Respondent enters a short free-text answer within the revision's character limit.
Respondent enters a longer free-text answer within the revision's character limit.
Respondent answers a bounded set of row prompts against one shared option scale; publication requires accessible non-table alternatives.

selection_rule: Multi-select per survey run; eligibility is derived from authoritative account relationships
field_description: Identifies each respondent group eligible for a survey run without turning the selection into a workspace role or user-controlled role switch.

field_option.option_description: Any active registered Massage Nexus member.
Any member in the platform's baseline client audience, whether or not the member has another role.
A client linked to an eligible completed booking or service event.
A verified or otherwise eligible therapist or practitioner derived from practitioner relationships.
A member with effective management access to an establishment, including an authorized owner or operator.
A member with effective management access to a seller or commercial product operation.
A member with an active or recently completed Massage Campus enrollment.
An eligible instructor or trainer in Massage Campus.
A member with effective administrative responsibility in Massage Campus.
A member with eligible contribution activity or an effective contributor assignment.
A member with effective editorial access.
A member with effective translation access or approved translation participation.
A member linked to an eligible advertiser or sponsorship account.

field_description: Identifies the channel through which one survey response was captured so native, Community Pulse, Microsoft Forms, paper, and controlled administrative-entry cohorts remain distinguishable.

field_option.option_description: Captured through a dedicated Massage Nexus survey form.
Captured one question at a time through the authenticated Community Pulse widget.
Imported from a preserved Microsoft Forms response export.
Entered from an original printed questionnaire under the paper-entry verification process.
Entered by an authorized administrator from another approved documented source.

field_description: Defines whether respondent identity is retained and who may connect it to answers; it does not change the public aggregate threshold.

field_option.option_description: Authorized survey staff may view the respondent identity with the response for an approved purpose.
The system retains a respondent link for eligibility and completion, while ordinary result viewers receive de-identified answers and aggregates.
No Massage Nexus user identity is stored with the response; external duplicate controls and methodology must be documented separately.

field_description: Identifies each approved interface placement where a survey run or Community Pulse question may be offered.

field_option.option_description: Community Pulse placement in the public homepage right sidebar.
Right-sidebar placement on approved Article, Review, News, or Guide pages.
Right-sidebar placement on approved establishment, practitioner, service, or product profile pages.
Right-sidebar placement near a Discussion page without becoming a user-authored Discussion poll.
Invitation or pinned task inside an eligible user's workspace.
Full survey form on its dedicated authenticated route.

field_description: Tracks the scheduling and participation lifecycle of one conducted period of an immutable survey revision.

field_option.option_description: Run settings may still be edited and no participation is accepted.
Approved run is waiting for its opening time.
Eligible respondents may submit or edit answers under the run rules.
Normal participation and editing have ended; retained responses remain analyzable.
The run was withdrawn from participation because of a material issue; retained data follows the recorded withdrawal decision.
The closed run is retained outside ordinary active administration views.

field_description: Tracks whether a respondent's run-specific response is in progress, complete, withdrawn, or invalidated.

field_option.option_description: At least one answer is saved but required completion has not been submitted.
The response satisfies the run's completion rule and has been submitted.
The respondent withdrew the response where withdrawal is supported.
Authorized review excluded the response because it was duplicate, fraudulent, ineligible, corrupted, or otherwise invalid.

field_description: Tracks validation and processing of an external survey-response import batch.

field_option.option_description: Source and mapping may still be prepared.
The source file and mappings are being checked without writing response records.
Validation passed and the batch is authorized for import.
Response and answer records are being written idempotently.
The batch finished and preserves imported and rejected counts.
The batch stopped because of an error and requires review before retry.
An authorized user cancelled the batch before successful completion.

selection_rule: Single-select when a run is triggered or scoped by another domain record
field_description: Classifies the optional domain context that caused or scopes an event-triggered survey run without storing a raw collection name as an ungoverned enumeration.

field_option.option_description: Survey is scoped to an eligible booking or completed service event.
Survey is scoped to a Massage Campus course, module, lesson, or program completion.
Survey is scoped to an eligible Support interaction or resolved ticket.
Survey is scoped to an approved research, outreach, event, or product campaign.
